operating_state: Consolidated revenue increased 10% to $16.2 billion in Q3 2021 driven by commercial aerospace recovery as aftermarket sales rose significantly across Collins Aerospace and Pratt & Whitney from normalized flight hours and fleet utilization. Pratt & Whitney delivered organic growth from higher commercial aftermarket shop visits and spare parts demand plus increased OEM engine deliveries. Defense segments maintained stable performance with international programs driving growth.
cost_structure: Operating profit margins improved to 8.3% versus 2.9% prior year driven by commercial aerospace volume leverage and favorable contract adjustments at Pratt & Whitney. Cost of sales improved to 80.7% from 88.2% due to favorable commercial aerospace aftermarket and OEM product mix at Collins Aerospace and absence of prior year unfavorable contract adjustments.
material_events: RTX completed $2.0 billion in share repurchases with $3.0 billion remaining under authorization and issued $2.0 billion in new debt to redeem existing 2022 maturity notes. The company completed sale of Forcepoint business for $1.1 billion net cash and entered agreements to acquire FlightAware and SEAKR Engineering for approximately $1.0 billion combined. Legal entity reorganizations generated deferred tax benefits while workforce reductions and facility consolidations target annual savings.
macro_exposures: U.S. federal contractor COVID-19 vaccination mandate requires employee vaccination by December 2021 with potential operational impacts from non-compliance among subcontractors and suppliers. Turkey sanctions related to Russian S-400 system acquisition could disrupt sole-sourced component supplies for aerospace operations. China announced potential sanctions against RTX related to Taiwan Foreign Military Sale of reconnaissance equipment.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued commercial aerospace recovery with full recovery potentially occurring in 2023 or 2024 supported by increasing flight hours and aftermarket demand though uncertainty remains regarding pace and timing. Defense bookings provide stable revenue visibility with substantial backlog. The company targets completion of majority workforce and facility cost reduction actions by 2022 expecting significant annual recurring savings.
uncertainty_profile: Contract liabilities include advance payments from Middle East customer for contracts RTX no longer believes executable due to regulatory approval challenges with potential refund obligations if contracts terminate. Pending regulatory approvals required for acquisitions and divestiture transactions. Proposed U.S. corporate tax rate increases would require revaluation of deferred tax assets upon enactment potentially resulting in material one-time noncash tax expense increases.
